title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Google App C2 Communication (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
description: Detects BLUELIGHT C2 communication via Google services. The variant uses custom Google App authentication with spoofed Firefox User-Agent and Korean language Accept-Language headers (ko-KR). Accesses Gmail and Google Account settings to enable IMAP for email exfiltration. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $chrome strings.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and ((('Command Line' like '*ko-KR,ko;q=0.8,en-US;q=0.5*' or 'Command Line' like '*rv:80.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/80.0*')) or ( (('Destination Hostname' like '*mail.google.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*myaccount.google.com*')) and not (('Process Name' like '*\\chrome.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\msedge.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\firefox.exe')) ) or (('Command Line' like '*AccountSettingsUi/data/batchexecute*' or 'Command Line' like '*BqLdsd*' or 'Command Line' like '*SNlM0e*'))) | stats count by 'Host Name (Server)', Entity, 'Destination Hostname'
falsepositives: Korean-language users browsing Google services with Firefox 80
The batch execute and SNlM0e patterns are highly specific
